This is a special word that shows an exception. In this ayah, it's saying 'no reward except...' connecting to the words مِنْ أَجْرٍ (any reward) that come before it.
This is the basic form meaning something high or elevated

Adding ى with small alif (ٰ) at the end turns this into a preposition showing something is on top of or dependent on something else. Here it connects to the word أَجْر (reward) to show what the reward depends on.

steps.m: to care for, develop, nurture to completion

steps.n: Doubling the middle letter ب with shadda shows this is someone who does this action perfectly. The fatha on ر shows this is a noun.
Adding kasra (ِ) at the end shows this word is connected to what comes after it (الْعٰلَمِينَ - the worlds/creation)
Forms a noun meaning 'that which is known/perceived', which came to mean 'world' since the world is everything we can know or perceive
Makes the word plural to refer to multiple worlds or realms of existence
Makes the word definite by adding ال with a sukun on the ل
Adds fatha at the end to show the word's role in the sentence
Basic vowels (fat-ha) added to make it past tense 'he came'
Changed to present tense form with ت prefix for 'you'
Changed ending to ون to show multiple people are doing the action
Added أَ at the beginning to make it a question
Forms the basic noun for a male person or being
Makes the word plural to mean multiple males
Adds definite article and doubles the first letter with shadda

steps.m: the males (as object of أَتَأْتُونَ - do you approach)
steps.n: Adds fatha at end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb أَتَأْتُونَ (do you approach)
This is the basic preposition that shows something comes from somewhere else
Added fatha at the end to connect it smoothly to the next word ٱلْعٰلَمِينَ (the worlds). This is like adding glue between words to make them flow better when speaking.
Added vowels to connect the letters and create a noun meaning something that makes something known or distinguishable
Adding alif creates a noun meaning the entire created world/universe
Added ين to make it plural (more than one world/universe)
Added ال (the) at the start and lengthened the first a sound with a long alif
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added ت at start and changed last vowel to ُ to make it present tense for 'you'
Added ون at the end to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: and you (all) leave/abandon (what Allah created for you)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at start to connect this word to what came before it. This word relates to what follows it in the ayah (what Allah created for you)
Basic interrogative particle meaning 'what'. In this ayah, it asks about what is being left/abandoned.
Added fatha mark to show clear pronunciation

steps.m: to create, make, or form something from nothing
he created (what رَبُّكُم your Lord created)
steps.n: Adding the three fathas (َ) changes this from a basic concept into a past tense verb meaning 'he created'. This word connects to رَبُّكُم (your Lord) who is doing the creating.
Added fatha to show the basic vowel of the preposition ل

steps.m: for/to you all (connected to أَزْوٰجِكُم - your spouses)
steps.n: Added the plural pronoun كُمْ (you all) to show who the preposition is directed to. This combines with أَزْوٰجِكُم (your spouses) later in the ayah to show what belongs to this group.
Adding the fatha vowel mark shows this is a past tense verb
The shadda combines two 'ب' letters to make the word mean 'Lord/nurturer' instead of the verb 'to nurture'
The damma shows this word is doing the action (subject), not being left/abandoned (it's the one who nurtures/sustains)

steps.m: your lord (object of تَذَرُونَ - you leave/abandon)
steps.n: Adding كُم shows this lord belongs to the people being addressed. In this ayah, it's what is being abandoned/left by them.
Basic preposition showing something comes from or belongs to something else
Added emphasis on م with shadda because it comes after a word ending in a vowel
Added sukun to show the ن has no vowel
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it into a noun meaning one part of a pair
Added أَ at start and changed internal vowels to make the plural form
Added كُم at the end to show these are the spouses belonging to all of you (plural you)
Starting with basic particle form that shows contrast or transition

steps.m: rather/but rather (contrasting with وَتَذَرُونَ - and you leave)
steps.n: Added fatha on ب and sukun on ل to show this is stopping and transitioning to a new statement that contrasts with the previous idea about leaving spouses
This is the basic form of the pronoun meaning 'you'. It's not derived from a root since it's a fundamental pronoun.
Adding ت makes it specifically refer to 'you' as a subject pronoun
Adding م with sukun (مْ) changes it from singular 'you' to plural 'you all', specifically referring to a group of males or mixed gender
Changed the root into a noun by adding fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter. This creates a collective noun for a group.
Added tanween damma to show this is the subject doing the action of being transgressors. This word is the subject of عَادُونَ (transgressors) in this ayah.

steps.m: to go beyond limits, to transgress

steps.n: Forms active participle pattern فَاعِل showing who does the action. This describes the kind of people they are (after قَوْمٌ meaning 'people').

steps.m: those who transgress limits (plural, describes قَوْمٌ)
steps.n: Adds masculine plural ending ون to show multiple transgressors. This describes the entire group of people (قَوْمٌ) who are being addressed.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense meaning
The و changes to ا because it's a weak letter
Adding وا changes 'he' to 'they'. In this ayah, this refers to the people speaking to Prophet Lot.
This is the basic conditional particle that shows something depends on something else

steps.m: if indeed (introducing the condition before لَّمْ تَنتَهِ)
steps.n: Added لَ at the beginning to add emphasis, like saying 'indeed' or 'truly' in English. This emphasizes the warning that follows.
This is a basic negative word that makes verbs mean 'did not' happen
Adding the shadda (ّ) makes the 'not' stronger, like saying 'definitely not' or 'not at all'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make basic past tense verb
Added reflexive pattern to show doing action to oneself
Changed to present tense with 'you' as subject

steps.m: you stop yourself (as condition: if you don't...)
steps.n: The ي at the end is dropped because it comes after 'if not' (لَئِن لَّمْ) - this is how verbs change in 'if' statements
Added ُ (damma) at the end to show this is the main subject being addressed
Added يٰ at the beginning to show we are calling out to Lot directly
Added basic vowels to connect the letters
The prefix تَ changes it from just 'being' to 'you are' or 'she/it is' in the present tense
The ل at the start makes it stronger - like saying 'surely' or 'definitely'

steps.m: surely you will be indeed (threat in response to يٰلُوطُ)
steps.n: The emphatic نّ (nun of emphasis) is added to further emphasize the certainty of the verb
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows where something comes from or is part of
Added fatha at the end to connect it smoothly to the next word ٱلْمُخْرَجِينَ (the expelled ones). This shows Lut would be one of those expelled.
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb

steps.m: those who are made to leave
steps.n: Changed from 'he left' to 'people who are forced to leave' by adding مُ at start and ين at end

steps.m: the ones who are made to leave (threatened to be done to لوط)
steps.n: Adding 'ال' (the) makes it refer specifically to Lut's people who were threatened with expulsion

steps.m: he said (introducing what follows: إِنِّى لِعَمَلِكُم مِّنَ ٱلْقَالِينَ)
steps.n: Added vowels to make it past tense. This word introduces direct speech that follows.
Changed قَوَلَ to قَالَ by replacing و with ا - this is a common change with weak letters
This is a basic emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger, like adding 'definitely' in English

steps.m: indeed I (emphasizes the speaker in قَالَ - he said)
steps.n: Added ي (written as ى) at the end to show 'I'. The نّ and ي combine to make نِّ. This connects to قَالَ (he said) to show who is speaking.
Added fatha vowels to connect the root letters, making a noun that means the actual work or deed itself
Added لِ at beginning to show purpose ('for/to'), and kasra at end shows it's connected to what comes after
Added كم at the end to show the work belongs to multiple people being addressed. In this ayah, this is actually being said to Allah using the royal/respectful plural form.

steps.m: from (showing that the speaker is part of ٱلْقَالِينَ - those who detest)
steps.n: This is a preposition showing where something comes from or what group someone belongs to

steps.m: from (showing that the speaker is part of ٱلْقَالِينَ - those who detest)
steps.n: Two changes happen here: 1) The م gets a shadda (ّ) because it comes after ى in إِنِّى, and 2) We add َ (fatha) at the end to connect smoothly to the next word ٱلْقَالِينَ
Changes to show who is doing the hating (adds فَا to make it 'the hater')
Makes the word plural
Adding ٱلْ makes the word definite ('the')
Adding fatHa case ending because this word is the object of the preposition مِنَ (from/among) in the ayah

steps.m: Lord (one who nurtures and cares completely)
steps.n: The doubled middle letter (shadda) makes this into a noun meaning 'the one who does this action perfectly'
Adding kasra at the end makes this into a call/plea to the Lord
Added shadda to make the meaning stronger and more intense. The final weak letter و changes to ى.
Removed the last letter and changed vowels to make it a command to one person, meaning 'you save!'
Added نِى at the end to show the command is to save 'me'. This is Lut speaking to his Lord.

steps.m: to belong to family, to take a wife/settle
family, relatives, household (object of نَجِّ - save)
steps.n: Root letters أ-ه-ل turned into the simple noun 'family' by adding fatha and sukun marks. This word shows who is being saved in the sentence.
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to another word coming after it
Adding conjunction prefix و meaning 'and'

steps.m: and my family (object of نَجِّ - save)
steps.n: Adding ى at the end shows possession - 'my family'. This whole phrase is what is being saved in نَجِّنِى وَأَهْلِى (save me and my family).
Combined the two basic words مِن (from) and مَا (what)
The ن of مِنْ combines with the م of مَا to make a stronger م sound (shown by ّ). This is just a pronunciation change, not a meaning change.
Added fathas to connect the letters and make it past tense
The يَ at the start shows it's happening now or in the future. The sukun (ْ) on the middle letter is needed for this form.
The ونَ ending shows many people (3 or more) are doing the action together
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter makes it mean 'to save someone else' instead of 'to escape oneself', showing more action
Changed the final weak letter to ي with sukun for joining with suffixes
Added نا to show 'we' did the action
Added هُ to show who was saved (him)
Added فَ to connect this action with what happened before

steps.m: to belong to a family, to take a wife

steps.n: Root letters got vowels to make the common noun meaning 'family'
Added fatha to show this word is the object (what was saved) in relation to the verb فَنَجَّيْنٰهُ (We saved)
Added pronoun هُ to show possession (his family)
Added conjunction وَ to connect with previous phrase, plus pronunciation marks
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Adding أ at start changes meaning to 'make something happen'
Added ين to show this describes multiple people (referring to the family mentioned in أَهْلَهُ)

steps.m: except (an old woman who remained behind)
steps.n: This is a common exception particle that shows something is different from what was mentioned before. In this ayah, it's excluding an old woman from those who were saved.
The و makes it mean someone who is very/completely in this state

steps.m: an old woman (object of إِلَّا - except)
steps.n: Adding ًا (tanween) at the end shows this word is being used as a direct object (something being excepted)
Basic preposition showing where the old woman was - among those who remained behind
The ي changes to ى at the end of words. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun describing someone who does this action
Added ٱلْ to make it specific
Changed from singular to plural form
Added fatha at the end because this word comes after the word في (in/among)

steps.m: then (connects this event to what happened before)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that shows one thing happened after another. In this ayah, it connects to the previous events, showing what Allah did next.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter makes the destruction more complete and intense
Added sukun to prepare for adding 'we' ending
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make them pronounceable
Adding long alif at start changes it from verb to comparative adjective meaning 'other'
The ين ending makes it refer to multiple people
Adding ال makes it specific. This word is what was destroyed (object) in the phrase 'Then We destroyed the others'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb

steps.m: he caused rain to pour down
steps.n: Adding أ at the start makes the verb causative - someone is making the rain fall rather than it falling naturally. The word مَّطَرًا is the direct object (in accusative case) showing what was sent down - the rain.

steps.m: we caused rain to pour down
steps.n: Adding نا shows that 'we' did the action. This is a verb, not a noun, so it doesn't have case endings.

steps.m: and we caused rain to fall
steps.n: Adding conjunction prefix و to connect with previous statement
This is a basic preposition showing something is on top of or above something else
The ى changes to يْ before adding pronouns at the end

steps.m: upon them (referring to the people who were punished)
steps.n: Adding هِم shows this applies to a group of people (them). This word connects to وَأَمْطَرْنَا (and We rained) to show what was rained upon them.
Changes from root letters to the word 'rain' by adding the vowel marks a-a
Adds emphasis to show this is intense or severe rain

steps.m: an intense rain (as punishment from وَأَمْطَرْنَا)
steps.n: Adds tanween alif to show this is the object of 'We rained down' (وَأَمْطَرْنَا)
Added short vowel marks (fat-ha) to make it mean 'became bad' in the past tense
The weak letter و changes to ء after alif

steps.m: and how evil was (describing مَطَرًا - the rain)
steps.n: Added فَ at the start to connect with the previous phrase about the rain, showing this is a comment about how bad that rain was
Adding the vowels creates the noun form for 'rain' itself
The ُ shows this is the subject doing the action in فَسَآءَ (how evil was)

steps.m: to warn or alert about danger

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to make it mean 'the person being warned' instead of 'to warn'
Added ين to make it plural, showing many people being warned

steps.m: the ones who are warned (referring to the people who received the warning in this story)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to specify these particular people who were warned

steps.m: indeed/truly (emphasizes فِى ذٰلِكَ لَءَايَةً that follows)
steps.n: Added shadda to strengthen the emphasis. This particle introduces and emphasizes the important statement that follows about there being a sign in what was mentioned.
Basic preposition showing location or position
Added ى at the end to complete the word. In this ayah, it connects 'that' (ذٰلِكَ) with what follows, showing 'in that' is a sign.
Basic demonstrative pronoun used to point to something nearby
The alif is written with a special mark (dagger alif) to extend the pointing range
Added ل with kasra to show distance

steps.m: that (pointing to something far) (refers to the punishment mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added ك with fatha to complete the word. In this ayah, it points back to the punishment mentioned in previous verses.

steps.m: to show, make clear, be apparent

steps.n: Root letters أ-ي-ي changed to form ءَايَ (sign/miracle) by adding vowels and joining letters
Added ة to make it a specific instance of a sign
Added لَ at the beginning to add emphasis, like saying 'surely' or 'indeed'
Added tanwin (ً) to show this is the object being discussed in the sentence after إِنَّ (indeed/verily)
This is a negative particle that negates what comes after it
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This negates the phrase that follows it: 'most of them were believers'
Added short vowels (fat-ha marks) to make it mean 'he was' in the past tense
Changed و to ا because it's a hollow verb (has weak middle letter)
Added أ at beginning and vowel marks to show this means 'more/most' of something

steps.m: most of them (refers to the disbelievers mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added هُم at the end to show who the 'most' refers to
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb about feeling safe
Changed to show who does the action by adding مُ at start and changing vowels
Adding shadda on م makes the meaning stronger and more emphatic
Added ين to show it's plural (many believers) and ِ to show it's being acted upon by the verb كَانَ (was)
This is an emphasis word that makes listeners pay attention to what comes next. The shadda (double letter mark) makes the emphasis stronger.

steps.m: and indeed (connects to رَبَّكَ - your Lord)
steps.n: The و connects this statement to what came before it. This word introduces an important statement about 'your Lord' (رَبَّكَ) that follows it.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters together
The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who does this action completely and perfectly
Adds كَ to show this is your Lord. The word is affected by إِنَّ which comes before it in the ayah, making it the thing being talked about
This is a basic pronoun that points back to 'your Lord' mentioned earlier in the ayah

steps.m: surely He is (referring to رَبَّكَ - your Lord)
steps.n: The لَ at the start adds emphasis to show certainty, connecting to إِنَّ earlier in the ayah to doubly emphasize that this statement about your Lord is definitely true
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past-tense verb
Adding ي in the middle makes this describe someone or something that has a lot of might/strength
Adding ال makes this refer specifically to Allah's attribute of being All-Mighty
Adding ُ shows this is the subject of لَهُوَ (indeed He is) in the phrase وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ (And indeed, your Lord - He is the All-Mighty)

steps.m: to be merciful, to care for others
very merciful (one who shows abundant mercy)
steps.n: Added ي between the second and last root letters with vowels to show someone who has a strong quality of mercy
Adding shadda makes the mercy even stronger. This word is one of Allah's names, showing His perfect mercy.
Adding ال makes the noun definite

steps.m: The Most Merciful (describing اللَّهِ, and following العَزِيزُ)
steps.n: The ُ shows this word follows العَزِيزُ (The Almighty) in describing Allah

steps.m: to deny, reject, or call something false

steps.n: Added vowels to make past tense 'he' form - means he did the action of denying
Added shadda (double letter) on ذ to show intensity. This word connects to أَصْحٰبُ (companions) who are doing the denying.
Added short vowels (fat-ha, kasra, fat-ha) to make it mean 'he stayed close' in the past
Changed from a verb to a noun meaning 'one who does the action'
Changed from single companion to plural companions by adding أ at the start and ا after ح - this is a common plural pattern in Arabic
Added ُ to show this is the subject doing the action in كَذَّبَ (denied/rejected). Changed ا to ٰ for style.
The basic word for a dense collection of trees
Added ة to show we're talking about one specific wooded area

steps.m: of the thicket (referring to the home of the people of Shuaib)
steps.n: Added ال (the) at the start to make it 'the thicket', and added a kasra (ِ) at the end to show possession
Added vowel marks to make past tense verb meaning 'he sent'
Changed from 'sent' to 'messenger' by adding مُ at start to show the person receiving/doing the sending
Made the word plural to show multiple messengers
Added 'the' to show these are specific messengers. This word is being acted upon by كَذَّبَ (denied) in this ayah.

steps.m: when (introducing the time Shuaib spoke to his people)
steps.n: This is a time particle that introduces a past event. In this ayah, it introduces when Shuaib spoke to his people, connecting to قَالَ (he said) in the next ayah.
This is the three-letter root that means speaking or saying
Added fathas to make past tense. This word tells what Shu'ayb did.
Changed قَوَلَ to قَالَ by replacing the weak letter و with ا, which is very common with hollow verbs
Added fatha to help with pronunciation when connecting to next part
Added هُ (him) to show who is being referred to

steps.m: to/for them (masculine plural) (who شُعَيْبٌ is speaking to)
steps.n: Added م with sukun to change 'him' to 'them', showing Shu'ayb is speaking to a group
Added vowel marks to show how to pronounce the name correctly: 'shu-ay-b'
Added double damma (tanween) at the end to show this is the subject (doer) of the verb قَالَ (he said) in this ayah
Basic negation word that means 'no' or 'not'

steps.m: will you not?/why don't you? (addressing those who شُعَيْبٌ is speaking to)
steps.n: Adding أَ at the beginning turns the negative statement into a question that encourages or urges the listener to do something. Here, Shu'ayb is urging his people to be mindful of Allah.

steps.m: to protect oneself from harm, to be mindful, to fear (consequences)
steps.n: Root meaning protection or guarding against harm
Root letters got fatha vowels to make past tense 'he' form, meaning 'he protected'
Changes from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and dropping وَ
Changes from 'he protects' to 'you protect' by replacing يَ with تَ
Adding ت with shadda shows doing the action thoroughly to oneself, making it more intense
Changes from 'you' (singular) to 'you all' by adding ون ending
This is a special word that adds emphasis to statements, like saying 'Listen!' before important news

steps.m: indeed I (speaking to رَسُولٌ أَمِينٌ - trustworthy messenger)
steps.n: Added ي at the end to mean 'I/me/my'. The ن gets a kasra (ِ ) sound before adding this ending. This shows that the speaker is emphasizing something about themselves.
This is a preposition that shows something is being directed to someone. Here it connects to رَسُولٌ (messenger) in the ayah.
Added كُمْ which means 'you all' when speaking to a group. In this ayah, it shows the messenger (رَسُولٌ) is being sent to all of you.
The و makes it mean someone who does this action as their main role
Added tanween to show this is indefinite (a messenger) and is the subject being described as trustworthy by the word أَمِينٌ that follows it
Added ي to show this is a quality or characteristic of someone
The tanween (ٌ) shows this is describing the messenger mentioned in the previous word رَسُولٌ (messenger)
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Added اِتَّ at the beginning to show doing the action to oneself. The middle ت gets a shadda because it combines with the first root letter و.
Added فَ at beginning to mean 'so' or 'then'. The اِ changes to ٱ after فَ.

steps.m: so protect yourselves! (command to a group)
steps.n: Changed to command form for multiple people by adding وا at the end and changing the vowels
Changed to the proper name for Allah by adding ال and merging letters

steps.m: Allah (object of فَٱتَّقُوا۟ - fear/be mindful of)
steps.n: The word 'Allah' gets a shadda mark on ل, a fatha ending because something is being done to Allah (He is the object), and a special alif at the start because it connects to the word before it
The root letters change to show someone who is doing the obeying
Adding أ at start changes meaning to 'make someone do the action'
Adding damma at end shows this is happening now (present tense)

steps.m: You all obey! (command to a group)
steps.n: Adding وا at the end makes it a command to multiple people
Adds نِ at the end to mean 'me' - telling a group 'obey me!'
Adds conjunction to connect with previous command فَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ (so fear Allah)
Added fatha to show this is a complete word. In this ayah, it means 'not' because it's followed by أَسْـَٔلُكُمْ (I ask you), making the phrase 'I do not ask you'.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it.
Changed the ا to آ, which is just a different way of writing the same sound. This doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'he asked' to present tense 'I ask' by adding أَ at start and ُ at end

steps.m: I ask you all (object of أَجْرِىَ)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show who is being asked. This word connects to أَجْرِىَ (my reward) in the ayah, showing what is being asked about.
This is a basic preposition showing something is on top of or over something else
Changed ى to ي with sukun to prepare for adding the pronoun. This is just a spelling change that happens before pronouns.
Added هِ at the end to show this means 'upon him'. The 'him' refers back to the reward (أَجْر) mentioned in the ayah.

This is a basic preposition showing where something comes from or what it's made of. In this ayah, it connects the word أَجْرٍ (reward) to the verb أَسْـَٔلُكُمْ (I ask you), showing what is being asked about.
Changes from root letters into a simple noun meaning reward
Added tanwin (double kasra) to show this is an indefinite noun (like 'a/any reward') after the word مِنْ (from)

steps.m: indeed (emphasizing that the speaker's reward is only from Allah)
steps.n: This is a basic particle that adds emphasis. In this ayah, it emphasizes that the speaker's reward comes only from Allah, connecting to إِلَّا (only) in the next part.

steps.m: to reward or give payment for work

steps.n: Changed from action to the thing itself (noun form)
Added kasra to prepare for possessive suffix
Added 'my' by adding ى with fatha. This word is what's being asked about in the phrase 'I do not ask you for'
This is a fixed word used to show exceptions, like saying 'except' or 'only' in English. It's not built from a root like most Arabic words.
This is a basic preposition that shows something is on top of or higher than something else. It doesn't come from a root.
Added vowel marks to show exactly how to pronounce the word - 'fatha' (َ) marks make the 'a' sound.
Added the small alif (ٰ) at the end to show this is connected to the word that comes after it (رَبِّ meaning 'Lord'). This mark helps show how words flow together when reading.

steps.m: to care for, nurture, be lord over

steps.n: Added fatha and doubled the middle letter to create the noun meaning 'Lord'
Adding kasra at the end makes it into a call/address to the Lord
Added vowels to connect letters and create a noun meaning something that helps you know or recognize things
Adding alif creates a noun meaning the entire created world/universe
Added plural ending to show multiple worlds or realms

steps.m: the worlds (object of رَبِّ - Lord of)
steps.n: Added 'the' at beginning and final vowel to show this describes whose Lord (رَبِّ) this is

steps.m: to complete or fulfill something fully

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Added أ (hamza) at start to make it mean 'to fulfill completely/perfectly' - this form shows doing something thoroughly
Changed to command form by dropping the final ى and changing the middle vowel

steps.m: fulfill! (all of you) (command to give full measure in الْكَيْلَ)
steps.n: Added plural marker to make it address multiple people. This is commanding them to give full measure in weights and measurements (الْكَيْلَ).
Changed from action to noun. This word is what's being fulfilled in the command 'fulfill the measuring.'
Added 'al' (ال) at the start to make it 'the measuring' - talking about measuring specifically in trade
This is the basic negative particle that negates the verb تَكُونُوا۟ (be) that follows it
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to the previous command أَوْفُوا۟ (fulfill)
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' are doing the action
Added وا at the end to show multiple people are being addressed
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows something coming from somewhere or belonging to a group
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly to the next word ٱلْمُخْسِرِينَ (those who give less). In this ayah, it means 'from among those who give less than they should'

steps.m: to give less than due/to cheat in measure
one who gives less than due measure
steps.n: The مُ prefix and ِ vowel work together to show who does the action, making it mean 'someone who cheats in measuring'

steps.m: the one who cheats in measuring
steps.n: The ال prefix makes the word definite
The ين ending makes it refer to multiple people. This comes after مِنَ (from) in the verse.

steps.m: those who cheat in measuring (object of تَكُونُوا/be)
steps.n: The َ shows this describes who you should not become (object of تَكُونُوا/be)
Adding وا at the end and putting ِ (kasra) after ز makes it mean 'you all weigh!' - a command for a group

steps.m: and weigh! (command to group) (connected to وَأَوْفُوا)
steps.n: Adding و connects this command to the previous command in the ayah (وَأَوْفُوا - and fulfill)
Changes verb into noun meaning the tool used for fair measuring
Added ال to make it specific - this is the particular scale being referred to

steps.m: with the scale (tool for وَزِنُوا۟ - weigh)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to show how the weighing should be done, and ِ at the end because it follows بِ. The ال changes to ٱلْ after بِ. This word explains how to do the weighing mentioned in وَزِنُوا۟ (weigh).
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changes verb 'to stand' into 'one who is straight/upright'
Added ٱلْ to make it specific ('the'), and ِ to show it describes the scale (ٱلْقِسْطَاسِ) mentioned in the ayah
Added fatha to show clear pronunciation
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this negative command with the previous one. This connects to the verb تَبْخَسُوا۟ (do not cheat/diminish) to form a series of commands.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show this happened in the past
The ت at the start shows it's addressing 'you' and the ُ at the end shows it's happening now

steps.m: you (all) decrease unfairly (the value of الناس's things)
steps.n: The وا shows it's addressing multiple people. This verb affects الناس (the people) and أشياءهم (their things) in the ayah.

steps.m: to be social or friendly with others

steps.n: Changes from verb (being social) to noun (people/humans)
Makes the word definite by adding 'the'
Adds fatha at end to show this word is the object of the sentence
Added vowels (fatha and sukoon) to the root letters to make the basic word for 'a thing'
This is a broken plural form meaning 'multiple things'
Added fatha at the end to show this word is the object of the verb تَبْخَسُوا (do not deprive/shortchange)

steps.m: their things (object of تَبْخَسُوا - do not deprive/shortchange)
steps.n: Added pronoun suffix meaning 'their' (masculine plural)
This is a negative particle that tells us the action تَبْخَسُوا۟ (defrauding/shortchanging) is not allowed or should not happen
The و connects this prohibition with previous commands or prohibitions in the passage. The whole phrase وَلَا تَبْخَسُوا۟ means 'and do not defraud/shortchange'
Added basic past tense vowels. The final و changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end.
Added تَ at start to show 'you' and changed vowels for present tense. The و returns in present tense.
Added ا with small و above to show multiple people are doing the action
Basic preposition showing location or place. In this ayah, it connects with الْأَرْضِ (the earth/land) to show where the corruption shouldn't happen.
Added ى at the end to complete the word. This is a spelling feature of Arabic writing that shows this is a complete preposition.
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to form the basic noun
Adding kasra at the end shows this word follows the preposition فِى (in) from the ayah
The word becomes definite with 'al' and takes the genitive case because it follows the preposition فِى (in) from the ayah
Added vowels to connect the letters together and show this happened in the past
The مُ at the start and ِ in the middle work together to show this is about someone who does the action

steps.m: people who damage things (describes how they act in تَعْثَوْا)
steps.n: The ين ending shows this is talking about many people. This word describes how the people are acting when they تَعْثَوْا (spread corruption) in the land.
Root و-ق-ي became اِتَّقَى by adding اِتَّ at start, which makes it mean 'to protect oneself' instead of just 'protect'. The و changed to ت and doubled (shadda)
Added وَ (and) at the beginning and changed اِ to ٱ because it comes after وَ

steps.m: and protect yourselves! (command to a group)
steps.n: Changed to a command for multiple people by adding وا at the end and changing the vowel to ُ
This is the base form of the relative pronoun that connects parts of a sentence, like 'who' or 'which' in English. In this ayah, it connects 'fear' with 'created you', showing who they should fear.
Added ٱلّ at the beginning to make it more specific, like changing 'who' to 'the one who'. The shadda (ّ) makes the ل sound stronger.

steps.m: to create, form, or bring into existence
he created (connected to الَّذِى - 'The One who')
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this is something that happened in the past. This word describes Allah (الَّذِى) in this ayah.

steps.m: created you all (connected to الَّذِى - 'The One who created you all')
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show who was created - in this case, all the people being addressed

steps.m: to form or create in large numbers

steps.n: The kasra vowels change this from a verb into a noun meaning natural qualities
Adding ة and shadda makes it a stronger noun meaning innate nature
Added fatha to show this is the object of خَلَقَكُمْ (created you)
Added definite article to specify this particular creation

steps.m: and the large creation/multitude (as object of خَلَقَكُمْ)
steps.n: Added conjunction to connect this with previous phrase

steps.m: to come first/be at the beginning

steps.n: The doubled middle letter makes it mean 'very first' or 'earliest'
The ين ending makes it refer to a group of people - in this case, the ancient peoples or earliest generations that came before
Definite article makes the noun definite

steps.m: the ancient ones/earliest generations (who were created before)
steps.n: The َ ending shows this word is connected to خَلَقَكُمْ (created you) in the ayah, referring to those who were created in earlier times
Adding vowel marks to make basic past tense verb
The root قَوَلَ changed to قَال and added وا at the end to show many people said it
Added optional marks: elongation sign (ـٓ) and small high rounded zero (۟) to indicate proper pronunciation. This word is the subject of إِنَّمَآ (indeed/only) in this ayah.
This is an emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows

Adding مَا changes the meaning from emphasis to restriction. The آ is just a writing variant of ا. This combination creates a fixed expression meaning 'only' that restricts what follows (in this case أَنتَ - you).
This is the basic form of the independent pronoun meaning 'you'

steps.m: you (masculine singular, being addressed by قَالُوٓا۟)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation. This pronoun is being used as the subject of what the speakers (قَالُوٓا۟) are saying.
This is a basic preposition that shows something comes from somewhere or belongs to a group
Added َ (fatha) at the end to connect smoothly to the next word الْمُسَحَّرِينَ (the bewitched ones). This shows that the person being talked about is from/of the bewitched ones.

steps.m: one who is affected by magic (object of مِنَ)
steps.n: The مُ prefix shows this is someone who receives the action of magic (like being bewitched), while the vowels help connect the root letters س-ح-ر together

steps.m: one who is deeply affected by magic (object of مِنَ)
steps.n: The shadda intensifies the meaning to show someone strongly affected by magic

steps.m: one who is deeply affected by magic (connected to مِنَ)
steps.n: The kasra ending shows this word connects to مِنَ (from/of)

steps.m: people who are deeply affected by magic (connected to مِنَ)
steps.n: The ين ending makes it refer to many people. It comes after مِنَ (from/of) in the ayah

steps.m: the people who are deeply affected by magic (connected to مِنَ)
steps.n: Adding ال makes the word definite
This is the basic negative particle that means 'not'. In this ayah, it's being used to negate what comes after it.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it

steps.m: and not (negating أَنتَ إِلَّا بَشَرٌ - you are but a human)
steps.n: The alif is lengthened to آ for emphasis in pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning, but shows this is a complete phrase before the next part of the sentence.

steps.m: you (subject of إِلَّا بَشَرٌ - 'except a human')
steps.n: This is one of the basic pronouns in Arabic, used when talking directly to one male person. In this ayah, it's being used to say that the person being addressed is just a human like everyone else.
This is a particle that shows exception or restriction. In this ayah, it connects 'you are' (أَنتَ) with 'a human' (بَشَرٌ) to mean 'you are nothing but/only a human'

steps.m: to be visible, to have outer appearance
steps.n: The root letters combine to give the basic meaning related to good news and pleasant appearance
Adding fathas creates a noun meaning 'human being' - referring to visible creatures with outer appearance

steps.m: a human being (subject of أَنتَ - you are)
steps.n: Adding tanween dammah (ٌ) makes it indefinite (a/an) and shows it's the subject. In this ayah, it's saying 'you are but a human being like us'

steps.m: to be like or similar to
steps.n: The root gives us the basic idea of similarity or resemblance
Root letters م-ث-ل got vowels (ِ and ْ) to make a noun meaning 'similar to' or 'like'
Added ُ at the end to show this describes the subject بَشَرٌ (human being)
Added نَا at the end to show possession/belonging - 'our' or 'us'
Added shadda on م to emphasize the sound, but meaning stays the same

steps.m: if (introducing condition for نَّظُنُّكَ/we think you)
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces the condition 'if' for the following statement 'we think you'

steps.m: and if (connecting to previous statement وَمَآ أَنتَ إِلَّا بَشَرٌ مِّثْلُنَا/and you are only a human like us)
steps.n: The و connects this condition to the previous statement 'and you are only a human like us', showing these ideas are related
The root gives us the basic meaning of thinking or believing something
Connected the root letters with fatha and added shadda to show basic past tense form
Added ن at start to show 'we' and changed vowels to show present tense
Added كَ at end to show 'you' as the object - who we are thinking about
The ن at the start got stronger (doubled) to add emphasis, meaning 'certainly/surely'
This is a basic preposition that shows something belongs to or comes from something else
Added a fatha at the end to help connect it smoothly with the next word (الْكٰذِبِينَ)
The لَ at the start adds emphasis, showing strong certainty. In this ayah, it emphasizes that the person is being accused of definitely being among the liars.
The root gives us the basic meaning of lying or being false

steps.m: a liar (object of نَظُنُّكَ - we think you)
steps.n: Root 'lie' becomes 'liar' by adding alif after first letter and kasra under second letter. The ٌ shows it's any liar, not specific

steps.m: liars (plural, object of نَظُنُّكَ - we think you)
steps.n: The ين ending makes it plural - more than one liar. This word is the object of نظن (we think/believe), showing who they think he is.

steps.m: the liars (object of مِنَ - from among)
steps.n: The ال makes it specific - these particular liars. The word follows مِنَ (from/among) in the phrase لَمِنَ الكَاذِبِينَ (surely among the liars)
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Adding أ at the start makes it mean causing something to happen instead of just happening
Changed from past tense 'he made fall' to a command 'you make fall!' by changing middle vowel and ending

steps.m: so make (something) fall! (command to make كِسَفًا fall)
steps.n: Added فَ at start to show this is a result or consequence. This is commanding to make pieces (كِسَفًا) fall from the sky.
Form used for feminine superlative (meaning 'highest')
The ى changes to ي with sukun when adding a suffix. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect meaning.

steps.m: upon us (object of فَأَسْقِطْ - so drop)
steps.n: Added نا at the end which means 'us'. This word is connected to فَأَسْقِطْ (so drop) - it shows what should be dropped upon.
Changed the verb into a plural noun meaning the resulting pieces from breaking something

steps.m: pieces (object of فَأَسْقِطْ/so cause to fall)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this word is receiving the action of falling in the phrase 'so cause pieces to fall upon us'
The shadda (ّ) doubles the م sound to add emphasis, like saying 'directly from' or 'truly from'
The fatha (َ) at the end connects this word to the next word السَّمَآءِ (the sky), showing it means 'from the sky'
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a basic past tense verb
Changed the verb 'to rise' into a noun 'sky' by adding a long aa sound and hamza at the end
Added ال (the) at the start and doubled the س with a shadda because it comes after ال
Added kasra at the end because it comes after مِنَ (from). Also lengthened the alif to آ for proper writing style.

steps.m: if (introduces condition for كُنتَ - you were)
steps.n: This is a basic conditional word that introduces a condition. In this ayah, it connects 'if you were' (كُنتَ) with what should happen in that case. It's like saying 'if X is true, then Y should happen'.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'he was' to a command 'be!' by shortening the word and changing the vowels. This is a simple command form.

The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in Arabic verbs with و in the middle

Adding vowel marks to make it a past tense verb meaning 'to know'

steps.m: to be or exist in a state or condition

Basic preposition showing location or position. In this ayah, it connects 'a sign' (ءَايَةً) with 'that' (ذٰلِكَ), showing where the sign exists.

Adding لِ changes it from pointing to something close to pointing to something farther away

steps.n: Adding كَ makes it clear we're pointing to something specific that was mentioned before in the conversation or text. In this ayah, it refers back to the destruction of the disbelievers that was mentioned earlier.

steps.m: to show, make clear, or be apparent

Adding tanween (double fatha) makes it indefinite and shows it's being used as an object
The لَ adds emphasis to show this is definitely or surely a sign
The ً (tanwin) shows this is indefinite (a sign vs. the sign) and is the object that إِنَّ (indeed) is referring to
Added fatha to show proper pronunciation. مَا is a versatile word that can mean 'not' or 'what' depending on context. Here it means 'not' because it's negating the verb كَانَ (was) that follows it.

Added short vowels (fat-ha marks) to make it mean 'he was' in the past tense

steps.m: he was (describes the state of أَكْثَرُهُم/most of them)

Added أ at beginning and vowels to show comparison, like saying 'more' or 'most' in English
Added ُ to show this is the subject (doer) of the verb كَانَ (was) in this ayah
Added هُم meaning 'them' to show who we're talking about
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun describing someone who does the action of believing
Forms regular masculine plural from singular مؤمن

steps.m: mighty one (description of رَبَّكَ - your Lord)
steps.n: Changed the pattern to show someone who has this quality very strongly. This word describes 'your Lord' in the ayah.

steps.m: the Mighty One (description of رَبَّكَ - your Lord)

steps.m: the Mighty One (description of رَبَّكَ - your Lord)
steps.n: Adding ُ shows this is the subject of the sentence

steps.n: This root relates to caring and nurturing

steps.m: to trust, feel safe, or be secure
to cause to feel secure, to believe

steps.m: they believe/have faith (present tense) (subject of لَا)

Added fatha at the end to show this word is receiving the action of يَرَوُا (they see)
Added يَ at the beginning to show it's happening now or in the future. The ْ (sukun) and ِ (kasra) help with pronunciation.

steps.n: Added فَ to show this happens after something else. In this ayah, it shows what happens after the disbelievers continue in their denial.

steps.m: and then it will come to them (referring to punishment)

Creates feminine noun indicating one instance of something happening suddenly. This word is what comes to them in the ayah.
Adding tanween alif makes it describe how the action happens - it will come to them suddenly
This is a pronoun that refers to a group of males
Added و with fatha (وَ) at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it in the ayah (فَيَأْتِيَهُم)
This is the basic form of the word without any vowel marks. It's a negative particle that makes something 'not' happen.
Added fatha (َ) on the ل to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'laa'
Adding ي at the start shows the action is happening now
The ُ (damma) shows this is about feeling or experiencing something
Adding damma at the end shows this is a complete present tense verb
Adding ون at the end changes 'he' to 'they' (masculine plural). This word is negated by لا in the verse.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense form
Changed to present/future tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added فَ to show this happens after something else
Added وا to show multiple people are doing the action
