steps.m: do/does/did? (asking about مُنظَرُونَ - being given more time)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation. In this ayah, it's asking if they will be given more time.
This is the basic personal pronoun meaning 'we' - it's used when a group of people are talking about themselves.
Added vowel marks to show this is the subject (doer) of the verb مُنظَرُونَ (being given respite/time). The ُ at the end shows it's doing the action.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb

steps.m: those being given time/being waited for
steps.n: Added مُ at start to make it about who receives the action, plus ون at end to make it plural

steps.m: ones being given time (subject of فَيَقُولُوا)
steps.n: The ون ending shows this is about multiple people being given time. It's the plural form of مُنظَر (one who is given time)
Changed the verb into a noun meaning the result of punishment
Added 'our' to show who the punishment belongs to
Added 'bi' at the start to show this is the means or instrument
Added 'fa' to connect this with what came before it

steps.m: is it with our punishment (that they hasten with يَسْتَعْجِلُونَ)
steps.n: Added 'a' at the start to make it a question. This word is connected to يَسْتَعْجِلُونَ (they hasten) in the ayah, showing what they are hastening with.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added يَسْتَ at the beginning to show someone is asking/seeking to rush (like saying 'trying to rush'), and changed the middle vowel

steps.m: they seek to rush (referring to the disbelievers asking about Allah's punishment)
steps.n: Added ونَ to show multiple people are doing this action
The verb already means 'they' - removing the vowel marks doesn't change the meaning. The ون ending shows it's 'they' in both cases
Connected the root letters and added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added ت at the end to show 'you' did the action
Added فَ at the beginning to show this happens after something else

steps.m: have you seen? (asking about something mentioned in previous ayahs)
steps.n: Added أَ at the start to turn it into a question ('have you seen?'). The spelling of hamza changed but means the same thing

steps.m: if (introduces condition for مَّتَّعْنٰهُمْ - We gave them enjoyment)
steps.n: This is a basic word that shows a condition is coming. It connects two ideas: if something happens, then something else happens.

steps.m: if (introduces condition for مَّتَّعْنٰهُمْ - We gave them enjoyment)
steps.n: Added the kasra (ِ) mark under the إ to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'in'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning stronger or more intensive
Adding نا at the end shows 'we' did the action

steps.m: we provided them with much enjoyment
steps.n: Adding هُمْ shows who received the action ('them')

steps.m: we provided them with much enjoyment
steps.n: The first letter gets doubled (shown by shadda) and the long 'aa' sound is written slightly differently
Changes from verb 'to pass' into noun meaning 'one year'

steps.m: years (object of مَتَّعْنٰهُمْ - we gave them enjoyment)
steps.n: Changed to plural form. This word shows how long the enjoyment lasted in the phrase 'we gave them enjoyment for years'

steps.m: years (object of مَتَّعْنٰهُمْ - we gave them enjoyment)
steps.n: Added a small diagonal line (fatha) at the end to show this word is receiving the action of 'giving enjoyment'
This is a connecting word that shows one thing happens after another. In this ayah, it connects to the word جَآءَهُم (came to them) to show something happened after something else.
Added alif with madda (آ) to show long vowel sound. The hamza from the root ء combines with the ي to form آ.

steps.m: he came to them (subject of what يُوعَدُونَ 'they were promised')
steps.n: Added هُم at the end to show who received the action. This word is connected to يُوعَدُونَ (they were promised) in the ayah.
Basic form of the word that can mean either 'what?' (as a question) or 'that which' (referring to something)
The shadda is added because this word comes after ثُمَّ. In this ayah, it refers to 'that which' they were being promised (يُوعَدُونَ)

steps.m: to be or exist in a certain state

steps.n: Adding the fatha mark makes this a past tense verb
Adding the second fatha completes the past tense verb pattern
Added وا at the end to show multiple people (they) did the action. This word is the subject of يُوعَدُونَ (they are promised) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to passive present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people are receiving the promise/warning
This is a basic particle that can mean 'what' or act as a negation. It has no root since it's a particle.
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
The long alif (آ) adds emphasis. In this ayah, it's used with the past tense verb أَغْنَىٰ to mean 'did not benefit/avail'

steps.m: to be rich or free from need

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding أَ at the beginning and changing the vowels makes this mean 'to cause someone else to be rich' instead of 'to be rich yourself'
The alif at the end is written specially (ى with a small alif above) because it's at the end of the word. This word is the main verb that tells us what didn't help 'them' (عَنْهُم)
This is a preposition that shows separation or distance from something. The sukun (ْ) shows where the word stops.
Added هُم at the end to show this applies to a group of males. This word is receiving the action of أَغْنَىٰ (availed) in this ayah.
This is the basic form of the word that asks about something or refers to something general
The shadda adds emphasis and connects this word to the verb يُمَتَّعُونَ (they were given enjoyment) in this ayah. It's asking about what they were given.
Adding the fatha (َ) makes it a past tense verb
Adding the second fatha (َ) completes the basic past tense verb pattern
Added وا at the end to show multiple people (they) did the action. This word is connected to يُمَتَّعُونَ (they were given enjoyment) in the ayah.

steps.m: to give pleasure, to provide enjoyment

steps.n: Basic past tense verb meaning 'he provided enjoyment' - formed from root letters م-ت-ع
Adding shadda (doubling) on middle letter makes the meaning more intense - like providing a lot of enjoyment
Changed from 'he gave enjoyment' to 'he receives enjoyment' by adding yu- at start and -u at end to show something is being done to someone
Adding plural masculine suffix ون to make the verb plural
This is a negative particle that negates the action in this ayah. It's saying 'We did not destroy...'

steps.m: and not (negates the verb أَهْلَكْنَا - destroyed)
steps.n: The و connects this statement to what came before, showing this is part of a larger message

steps.m: and not (negates the verb أَهْلَكْنَا - destroyed)
steps.n: Added vowel mark on م and lengthened the final vowel for emphasis in recitation, without changing the meaning
Added fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Adding أ at the beginning changes the meaning from 'to perish' to 'to cause to perish/to destroy'
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action
This is a basic preposition that connects to قَرْيَةٍ (town/city) in this ayah, showing which towns are being talked about. It's like saying 'We did not destroy [any] from [the] towns...'
Changes from root meaning into a concrete noun for a place where people settle
Added tanween kasra to show this is the object of the preposition مِن (from) in the phrase 'We did not destroy any village'
Basic form of the exception particle. Not derived from a root.
Adding shadda emphasizes the exclusion meaning

steps.m: except (in this ayah: except that they had warners)
steps.n: Adding alif at the end helps connect this word smoothly with what follows. This word introduces an exception to the previous statement about destroyed towns - they were only destroyed after having warners.
This is a basic preposition showing something is directed towards or meant for something else. Here it connects to قَرْيَةٍ (town/village) mentioned earlier.

steps.m: for it (referring to قَرْيَةٍ - the town/village)
steps.n: ها refers back to قَرْيَةٍ (town/village) which was mentioned earlier in the ayah. Since قَرْيَةٍ is feminine in Arabic, we use the feminine pronoun ها.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Adding مُ at the start turns the verb into a noun meaning 'one who does the action'
Adding ون makes it plural (more than one)
Added the vowels ِ and ْ to make it into a noun meaning 'the act of remembering'
Added ىٰ at the end to make it mean 'a reminder' instead of just 'remembrance' - like turning 'remember' into 'reminder'
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation of the negation particle ما
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this phrase to the previous word ذِكْرَىٰ (reminder)
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense form
Changed from past tense 'he was' to a command 'be!' by changing vowels and making the word shorter
Added shadda to emphasize the command
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did this action in the past
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: one who wrongs others (predicate of كُنَّا - we were)
steps.n: Changed the word to show who does the action. The alif (ا) makes it mean 'someone who does wrong' instead of just the action of doing wrong.
The ين ending shows this refers to many people who do wrong. It's connected to the word كُنَّا (we were) to say 'we were wrongdoers'.

steps.m: not/what (introduces a negative statement or question)
steps.n: This is a basic particle that can either negate something (meaning 'not') or ask about something (meaning 'what'). In this ayah, it's used with the meaning 'not' to deny that devils brought down the Quran.

steps.m: and not (connects to previous statement while negating what follows)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this negative statement to what came before it. It's like saying 'and' + 'not' to connect two ideas while denying the second one.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
The تَ prefix and doubled ز show this is happening gradually or repeatedly. This word describes how the devils (الشَّيٰطِينُ) descended.
The تْ at the end shows this is referring to a feminine subject or a group of non-human things (in this case, the devils/الشَّيٰطِينُ)
Added kasra to show this is a preposition meaning with/by
Added the pronoun ه (him/it) with kasra to show connection. This word shows how the devils (الشَّيٰطِينُ) did not bring down the Quran.

steps.m: to act like a rebellious one
steps.n: Added ي after first letter to make it mean 'one who is rebellious and distant'
Added alif to make it into a noun meaning someone who does this action
Added ين to make the word plural (more than one devil)
Added ال at beginning to make it specific and ُ at end to show it's doing something in the sentence
This is a basic negative or question word that can mean either 'not' or 'what' depending on context. In this ayah, it means 'not' because it's negating what comes after it.
Added و at the beginning to connect this negation to the previous statement. This وَمَا is negating يَنۢبَغِى (it is suitable) in this ayah.

steps.m: to transgress, go beyond proper limits, seek inappropriately

steps.n: Root letters connected with vowels to make past tense verb meaning 'he transgressed'
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Adding ن changes meaning to 'being appropriate' rather than 'transgressing'
Added fatha to show the basic vowel sound of the preposition

steps.m: for them/to them (speaking about multiple males)
steps.n: Added هُمْ which shows this applies to multiple males. This word connects to يَنۢبَغِى (it is fitting) to show for whom something is fitting.
This is a negative particle that makes the following verb يَسْتَطِيعُونَ (they are able) into 'they are not able'
The و connects this phrase to what came before it, like saying 'and' in English

steps.m: to be able, to have capability
steps.n: The root carries the basic meaning of willing obedience or ability
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from past to present by adding يَ at start and changing middle letter from ا to ي

steps.m: he is able (with emphasis on capability)
steps.n: Added سْتَ to make it mean 'having the ability/capability to do something' rather than just 'obeying'
Added plural ending to show multiple people
This is a special word that makes what comes after it stronger or more certain, like adding 'definitely' in English.
Added هم which means 'they' (for a group). This word connects to لَمَعْزُولُونَ (are prevented) in the ayah.
This is a basic preposition that shows something is moving away from or being separated from something else
Added kasra at the end to connect it with the next word ٱلسَّمْعِ (the hearing). In this ayah, it shows that they (the devils) are kept away from hearing.
Changes from verb 'to hear' into the noun 'hearing'

steps.m: the hearing (object of عَنِ - away from)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific. The ِ at the end shows it follows the word عَنِ (away from). The shadda on س shows emphasis. In this ayah, it's saying they are kept away from hearing.
Changed to show something that has been removed or separated by others (passive form)
Added ون to show this describes multiple people who are removed/separated

steps.m: surely they are removed/separated (from السَّمْعِ - hearing)
steps.n: Added لَ at the start for emphasis, showing they are definitely separated. In this ayah, it's specifically saying they are separated from hearing (السَّمْعِ).
This is the basic negative particle that tells us something is not happening or should not happen

steps.m: so/then not (connects to تَدْعُ - call upon)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to connect this negative command to what came before it. This فَ shows that not calling upon other gods is a result or consequence of something mentioned earlier.

steps.m: to call out to, invite, pray to

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and changed و to ا because it's at the end of a past tense verb
Changed from 'he called' to 'you call' by adding ت at start. The middle letter got quiet (sukun) and last letter changed to و with a damma
The final و is dropped because it comes after لا (don't), which makes it a negative command
This is a basic preposition that shows doing something together with someone or something. In this ayah, it connects to اللَّهِ (Allah) to show not to call upon other gods with Allah.
Added ال and merged it with the word, making it a proper noun referring specifically to Allah. The hamza from إِلَه is dropped in this special word.
Added ِ at the end to show this connects to the word مَعَ (with) before it. Also added shadda on ل and changed initial alif to wasl form since it comes after another word.

steps.m: to worship or take as a deity

steps.n: Changed from a root meaning 'to worship' into a noun meaning 'a god' that can be worshipped

steps.m: a god (object of تَدْعُ - do not call upon)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this is the object being acted upon. This word is what's being called upon/invoked in the phrase 'do not call upon' (فَلَا تَدْعُ).

steps.m: to delay or come after something
steps.n: Connected the root letters that mean delaying or coming after something
Added long alif after hamza and fatha marks to create a pattern meaning 'another' or 'other'
Added fatha at the end to show this is describing إِلٰهًا (deity) in the phrase 'do not call upon another deity with Allah'
Added basic vowels to connect the letters and form a noun meaning
Added تَ at start to show 'you' and ُ at end to show present tense

steps.m: so you would be (result of لَا تَدْعُ - do not call upon)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this is a result of the previous command. Changed final vowel to فتحة to show this is part of a sequence of results.
This is a basic preposition showing source, origin, or belonging to a group
Added َ (fatha) at the end to connect it smoothly to the next word ٱلْمُعَذَّبِينَ (the punished ones). This combination means 'among the punished ones'
Added fatha vowels and doubled the middle letter to show intense punishment
Added مُ at start to change from 'punished' to 'person being punished'
Added ين to show this refers to multiple people being punished

steps.m: the severely punished ones (among those who will be punished)
steps.n: Added ال to specify these particular punished ones. The word comes after مِنَ (from/among) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to a command form by adding أ at start, changing middle vowel to kasra (ِ), and adding sukun (ْ) at end
Added و to connect this command to what came before. This command is specifically about warning عَشِيرَتَكَ ٱلْأَقْرَبِينَ (your close relatives)
Forms a noun meaning 'one who is closely associated with others'
Adds ة to make it refer to a whole group rather than one person
Adds كَ to show possession (your) and تَ as a connector. This word is what is being warned in the phrase 'warn your clan'
Added vowels to make it mean 'he was close' - like adding -ed in English to show it happened in the past
Added أ at the beginning to make it mean 'closer/closest' instead of just 'close', like turning 'close' into 'closer' in English
Added ين at the end to make it mean 'closest relatives/family members' (plural)

steps.m: the closest relatives (object of أَنذِرْ - warn)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and show these are particular close relatives. This word is the object of the verb أَنذِرْ (warn) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
The imperative form tells someone to do the action

steps.m: and lower! (as a command to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: The letter و (wa) meaning 'and' was added to connect this command with previous instructions
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
The word became a noun meaning the part of a bird that helps it lean/incline in flight. In this ayah, it metaphorically refers to being humble and gentle.
Added 'your' at the end. The word is being used metaphorically in the command 'lower your wing' to mean 'be humble and gentle'.
Added kasra at the end to show this word is being acted upon by something else
Added لِ at the beginning to show this is 'for' whoever. This word connects to اتَّبَعَكَ (followed you) in the ayah, showing who the action applies to.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added اِ at start and doubled the middle letter with shadda to show doing something with extra effort or purpose
Added كَ at the end to show who was followed. This word is the object of the command وَٱخْفِضْ (lower/extend) in this ayah.
Changed the writing style of the initial alif from اِ to ٱ, which is common in Quranic text. This doesn't change the meaning or pronunciation.
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows where something comes from or its source
Added fatha at the end to connect it smoothly to the next word ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (the believers). This is like adding glue between words to make them flow better when speaking.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past-tense verb
Changes from 'he believed' (past tense verb) to 'believer' (person who believes) by adding مُ at start
Made plural to show many believers. The word is in genitive case because it comes after the preposition مِنَ (from/of).
The word changed to 'the believers' by adding ال (al) at the start, making it specific to a particular group

steps.m: if (introduces the condition عَصَوْكَ - 'they disobey you')
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces a possible scenario. Here it introduces the condition 'if they disobey you'

steps.m: and if/then if (connects the condition عَصَوْكَ to what comes after فَقُلْ)
steps.n: The فَ prefix shows this condition connects to what comes after it (فَقُلْ - 'then say'). The full meaning is 'and if they disobey you, then say...'
Connected the root letters with fathas. The final ي changes to ى because it's a weak letter at the end.
Added و to show multiple people did the action

steps.m: they disobeyed you (in response to فَإِنْ - 'and if')
steps.n: Added كَ to show who was disobeyed. This word comes after 'and if' (فَإِنْ) in the ayah, showing a conditional statement.
Changed to a simple command meaning 'say!' by adding a u-vowel (ُ) on first letter and making last letter silent (ْ). The middle و disappears.

steps.m: then say! (responding to عَصَوْكَ - they disobeyed you)
steps.n: Added فَ at beginning to show this is what to do after they disobey. This connects it to the previous word عَصَوْكَ (they disobeyed you).
This particle makes what comes after it stronger or more certain
The نّ takes kasra to prepare for the coming ي

steps.m: indeed I/truly I (speaking before بَرِىٓءٌ/free from)
steps.n: The ى at the end shows this is about me (I/my). This word connects to بَرِىٓءٌ (free from) that comes after it.
Added vowels to make it mean 'he became free' in the past tense
Changed from a verb (did something) to a noun (someone) by adding ي to describe a person who is free/innocent of something
Added ٌ at the end to show this is a subject. The ى with ٓ makes the word stronger, like saying 'completely free' instead of just 'free'
This is a basic word that shows where something starts or comes from
Added ما which means 'what' to specify what we're talking about
The ن and م combine and become one strong م (with shadda). This is a common change when these letters come together. The word connects to تَعْمَلُونَ (you do) to complete its meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, sukun on first root letter, and damma at end
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by replacing يَ with تَ at the start
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action

steps.m: to rely on or entrust something to someone

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter shows doing something thoroughly or completely

steps.m: he put his complete trust (in someone)
steps.n: Adding تَ shows doing the action to or for yourself - here it means putting your own trust in someone

steps.m: and put your complete trust (in عَلَى ٱلْعَزِيزِ ٱلرَّحِيمِ)
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this word to what came before it, like saying 'and'

steps.m: and put your complete trust! (in عَلَى ٱلْعَزِيزِ ٱلرَّحِيمِ)
steps.n: Changing the last vowel to sukun makes this a command - like saying 'Do it!'
This is the basic form meaning something high or elevated. It's a particle without a root.
Adding ى at the end turns this into a preposition showing dependence or reliance. Here it connects 'rely' with 'the Mighty', showing who to rely upon.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Adding ي in the middle makes this describe someone/something that has this quality very strongly
Adding ال makes this refer to Allah specifically as The Mighty One

steps.m: of The Mighty One (connected to عَلَى)
steps.n: Adding kasra at the end shows this word follows عَلَى (upon) in the ayah. The alif at the start changes to a different form (ٱ) when connecting to the previous word.
Added ي between second and third root letters to show this is a quality that is part of someone's nature

steps.m: The One who shows abundant mercy (attribute of العَزِيزِ)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it definite and shows this is referring to a specific divine attribute

steps.m: The One who shows abundant mercy (attribute of العَزِيزِ)
steps.n: The ِ shows this word follows العَزِيزِ in the sentence

steps.m: The Intensely Merciful One (attribute of العَزِيزِ)
steps.n: The doubled letter (shadda) makes the word stronger, showing very intense mercy

steps.m: The one who (refers to Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: This is a special connecting word that points to someone or something specific. Here it refers to Allah who 'sees you when you stand' (يَرَىٰكَ حِينَ تَقُومُ). The ٱلَّ at the beginning makes it specific, like saying 'the one' instead of just 'one'.

steps.m: to see or perceive with eyes or mind
steps.n: Basic past tense form of the verb
Added يَ at beginning to show present tense and 'he' as subject. The root letter ي at the end changes to ى due to being a weak letter.
Added alif maksura with superscript alif (ىٰ) to show this is the proper spelling of the weak letter ending
Added كَ at the end to show that 'you' are the one being seen
This word is used to talk about time or when something happens
Added kasra under first letter to make it into a noun meaning a specific time or moment
Added fatha at the end to show this word is in the accusative case
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense form
The middle letter gets a ُ (u) sound and becomes longer to make it present tense, like many similar words with و in the middle
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' or 'she' as the doer. This verb comes after حِينَ (when) in the ayah

steps.m: to turn, flip, or change position

steps.n: Doubling the middle letter makes the action more intense and repetitive

steps.m: moving about, going back and forth
steps.n: Adding تَ shows the action is being done by oneself - like saying 'to move oneself about'
The كَ (ka) was added at the end to mean 'your', changing it from just 'moving about' to 'your moving about'
The وَ connects this to the previous phrase, showing these actions happen together
Basic preposition showing location or position. In this ayah, it connects 'your movement' with 'those who prostrate'.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it's at the end of a word. This is just a writing change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changes from 'he prostrated' to 'one who prostrates' by using the pattern فَاعِل (faa3il) - like changing 'wrote' to 'writer'
The ين ending makes it plural for masculine beings. The word is in genitive case because it comes after the preposition في (in/among).
Adding ٱلْ makes it specific - 'the' prostrators rather than just any prostrators
This is a common word used to emphasize what follows, like saying 'truly' at the start of a sentence
The هُ at the end means 'he' - it connects to هُوَ (he) in the next part of the ayah

steps.m: he (referring to Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: This is one of the basic pronouns in Arabic that points to someone. In this ayah, it refers back to Allah, connecting 'Indeed He (Allah)' to 'He is the All-Hearing, All-Knowing'
Adding vowels makes this a past tense verb meaning 'to hear'
Changed from a past tense verb (he heard) to an intensive form meaning 'all-hearing' by adding ي and changing the pattern
Added ال to make it definite, referring specifically to Allah as The All-Hearing One

steps.m: The All-Hearing (attribute of Allah, subject of the sentence)
steps.n: Added ُ to show this is the subject of the sentence

steps.m: One who is all-knowing (attribute of Allah)
steps.n: Added ي between second and third root letters with vowels to show this is an intensive quality. When used for Allah, it means His knowledge is perfect and complete.

steps.m: The All-Knowing (attribute of Allah, paired with السَّمِيعُ/The All-Hearing)
steps.n: Adding ٱل makes it specific/definite. The ُ shows this is paired with السَّمِيعُ (The All-Hearing) as two attributes of Allah.
This is the basic form of the question word that turns a statement into a question

steps.m: shall/will (asking about أُنَبِّئُكُمْ - I inform you)
steps.n: Added sukun at the end to show this is a complete question word. This word is asking about the verb أُنَبِّئُكُمْ (I inform you) that follows it.
Added basic vowels to connect the letters
Doubling middle letter creates intensive meaning
Changed from 'he told' to 'I tell' by adding أُ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: I (will) inform you all (in response to هَلْ/shall I?)
steps.n: Added كُمْ meaning 'you all'. The هَلْ before this word makes it a question 'Shall I inform you?', and implies future tense.
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows something is on top of, upon, or about something else
The small alif (ىٰ) at the end shows this is connected to what comes after it in the sentence (مَن - 'who/whom'). It's asking about who the devils come down upon.
This is a question word asking about which people. In this ayah, it's asking about who the devils descend upon.
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
The doubled middle letter shows this happens multiple times or with extra force
Adding تَ shows this happens on its own, without someone else causing it

steps.m: it descends by itself repeatedly (subject of the sentence)
steps.n: The ُ ending shows this word is doing the action in the sentence

steps.m: one who is extremely rebellious and distant
steps.n: Added letters to make a noun meaning 'one who is very rebellious' - like turning 'rebel' into 'rebel person'
Added ين to make the word refer to multiple devils/satans
Added 'the' (ال) at the start and changed the spelling slightly, but means the same thing - devils/satans
Added ُ to show this is the subject doing the action in the sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding shadda makes the action happen multiple times or with more force

steps.m: to come down gradually/repeatedly by itself
steps.n: Adding ت shows the action happens to itself, like something descending on its own
Adding ُ at the end changes it from past to present tense
This is the basic form showing the concept of being high or elevated

steps.m: upon (preposition showing something is on top of أَفَّاكٍ)
steps.n: Adding ى at the end turns this into a preposition that shows one thing is on top of another. Here it shows the action تَنَزَّلُ (descends) happens upon أَفَّاكٍ (the liar).

steps.m: upon (preposition showing something is on top of أَفَّاكٍ)
steps.n: The small alif (ٰ) is added as a pronunciation guide to show the long 'aa' sound at the end

steps.m: to be complete, to encompass everything

steps.n: The doubled ل with damma creates a word meaning 'every' or 'all', showing completeness
The kasra at the end shows this word is connected to أَفَّاكٍ (liar) that comes after it, making it mean 'every liar'

steps.m: to turn away from truth, to deceive

steps.n: Basic past tense pattern (fa3ala) turns the root letters into 'he turned away'
The shadda (ّ) and alif (ا) show this person does this action repeatedly or as a habit
The tanween kasra (ٍ) shows this word is describing 'every' (كُلِّ) in the ayah

steps.m: to commit a sin or wrongdoing

steps.n: Changed root letters into adjective by adding vowels - describes someone who sins
Added ي to show this is someone who sins frequently or intensely
Added tanween kasra to show this is describing the word أَفَّاكٍ (liar) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Added يُ at start to show 'he' in present tense, and changed middle vowel to make it mean 'throws' now instead of 'threw'

steps.m: they throw (the subject of يُلْقُونَ ٱلسَّمْعَ - they cast their hearing)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. This word connects to السَّمْعَ (hearing) which is what they are throwing/casting.
Root meaning 'hear' becomes the noun 'hearing' by adding specific vowels
Added ٱل (the) and doubled the س with shadda to connect the sounds smoothly

steps.m: the hearing (object of يُلْقُونَ - they cast)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb يُلْقُونَ (they cast)
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Adding أ at the start makes it mean 'more/most'
The word 'most' gets 'of them' (هُمْ) attached to it, making it 'most of them'

steps.m: and most of them (subject of كٰذِبُونَ)
steps.n: Adds conjunction prefix و meaning 'and'
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changes from describing the action to describing the person who does it
Adds the masculine plural ending to show multiple liars
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Changed from verb (to compose poetry) to noun (poet) by adding ا after first letter
Special plural form for people who do professions or roles
Adding ال makes it refer to specific poets
Added و to connect with previous statement and final ُ to show this is the subject

steps.m: to follow or go after something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed from past to present by adding يَ at start and changing vowels - shows ongoing action
Added emphasis by doubling the ت with a shadda and changing middle vowel to kasra

steps.m: he follows them intensely (the misguided ones follow the poets)
steps.n: Added هُمُ to show who is being followed. This connects to ٱلْغَاوُۥنَ (the misguided ones) who are following ٱلشُّعَرَآءُ (the poets)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed from verb (to stray) to noun meaning 'strayer'
Regular sound masculine plural ending added to make it plural

steps.m: the ones who stray (subject of يَتَّبِعُ)
steps.n: Adding 'al' (ال) makes it 'the' straying ones - specifically those who follow poets
This is the basic emphasis word that adds strength to what comes after it
Adding أَ at the start turns this into a question that expects the answer 'yes'

steps.m: have you not...? (addressing the Prophet ﷺ)
steps.n: Adding مْ makes the verb that follows negative and changes the time to past. This word is asking the Prophet ﷺ about something obvious that he has already seen.

steps.m: to see with eyes or mind

steps.n: Added vowels to make the past tense verb
Added ت at beginning to show 'you' and changed last vowel to show present tense
The hamza and vowel at the end change to ى because it's a weak letter at the end

steps.m: you see (after أَلَمْ meaning 'do you not see?')
steps.n: The ى at the end falls off when أَلَمْ comes before it, making it a shorter word with the same meaning
This is a particle that emphasizes what comes after it, like adding 'indeed' or 'certainly' in English

steps.m: that they (emphasizes what follows in وَادٍ يَهِيمُونَ)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show this refers to 'they'. This connects to the later words وَادٍ يَهِيمُونَ (wander in every valley)
This is a preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. In this ayah, it shows the poets are wandering in valleys.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it's at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
The doubled ل (shadda) makes the meaning stronger to show it means absolutely everything. The damma (ُ) sound at the start makes it into a noun.
The kasra (ِ) at the end shows this word connects to وَادٍ (valley) that comes after it, making it mean 'of every valley'

steps.m: to flow or move through a path
steps.n: This root is related to movement, especially water flowing, which is why it came to refer to valleys where water flows
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a noun referring to the path itself rather than the action of flowing
The word for 'valley' gets a special ending (ٍ) to show it's any valley, not a specific one. It's used with 'every' (كُلِّ) in the ayah.

steps.m: to wander without purpose or direction

steps.n: Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowels. The middle alif changes to ي because it's a hollow verb.

steps.m: they wander/are wandering (referring to the poets mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action. This refers back to the poets (الشُّعَرَاء) mentioned earlier in the verse.

steps.m: that/indeed (used to emphasize what comes after)
steps.n: This is a common particle used to introduce and emphasize statements. It makes what comes after it more certain or important.
The و at the start connects this with what came before it, like adding 'and' in English

steps.m: and that they (subject of يَقُولُونَ - they say)
steps.n: The هُمْ at the end refers to 'they' or 'them' when talking about a group. In this ayah, it refers to the people who يَقُولُونَ (say) what they don't do.
Added basic past tense form with fathas
Changed to present tense with يَ prefix and internal vowel changes
Makes the verb plural for multiple male speakers

steps.m: they say (subject of مَا لَا يَفْعَلُونَ)
steps.n: Added ونَ to show multiple people. This word is connected to يَفْعَلُونَ (they do) in the ayah, showing these are the same people who aren't doing what they say.

steps.m: that which (connects يَقُولُونَ with لَا يَفْعَلُونَ)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that links what someone says with what they do (or don't do). In this ayah, it shows the contrast between what people say versus what they actually do.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it mean the opposite - like adding 'not' in English. Here it changes 'they do' to 'they do not'
Added the fatha mark (َ) to show how to pronounce it as 'laa'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, sukun on middle letter, and ُ at end
Added ونَ to show this is about a group (3 or more) doing the action

steps.m: except (introduces the believers who are excluded from the previous criticism)
steps.n: This is a basic particle that shows exception. In this ayah, it excludes the believers (ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟) from those who were criticized in previous verses.
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ذ letter to connect them smoothly

steps.m: those who (plural, refers to believers in ءَامَنُوا۟)
steps.n: Added ين at the end to show we're talking about multiple people. This word connects to ءَامَنُوا۟ (they believed) in the ayah, describing the believers.
Adding alif makes it about having faith in something/someone

steps.m: they believed (subject of الَّذِينَ - those who)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show many people did this action. This word describes what 'those who' (الَّذِينَ) did in this ayah.
Adding vowels makes it a past tense verb
Changed from singular (he) to plural (they) by adding وا at the end
Added و at beginning to connect this action with the previous word ءَامَنُوا۟ (they believed)
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changed pattern to show someone/something that has this quality
Added feminine plural ending to refer to multiple righteous things or deeds
Added ٱل to make it specific, doubled the ص, and added ِ to show it's the object of the verb عَمِلُوا۟ (they did/performed)
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added وا at the end to show many people did this action

steps.m: and they remembered/mentioned (connected to ءَامَنُوا۟ and عَمِلُوا۟)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this with the previous actions of believing and doing good deeds
Added ال to make it specific and changed the first letter, making it a proper noun for Allah

steps.m: Allah (as the object being remembered in وَذَكَرُوا۟)
steps.n: Added shadda on ل to emphasize it, changed initial alif to wasl form, and added fatha at end to show Allah is the object being remembered
Root letters got vowels (َ ِ) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'it increased'
Added ي to show this is a quality or characteristic
Added ًا at the end to show this describes how Allah was remembered - they remembered Allah much
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added ٱنت at the beginning to show the action is done for oneself
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did the action
Added و at beginning to connect this action with the previous one (remembering Allah)
This is a preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. Here it connects with the word بَعْدِ (after) to mean 'from after'.
The root letters were connected with vowels (َ and ْ) to make the word mean 'after'

steps.m: after (connected to مَا ظُلِمُوا۟ - what they were wronged)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this word connects to what comes after it in the ayah (مَا ظُلِمُوا۟ - what they were wronged)

steps.m: that which (connects ظُلِمُوا۟ to what came before)
steps.n: This word connects the verb ظُلِمُوا۟ (they were wronged) to the previous phrase, showing what happened after. It works like 'that which' in English.
This root represents the basic idea of treating someone unfairly or causing harm to them
Connected the letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed the vowels to show that someone received the action instead of doing it
Added وا at the end to show that many people were wronged, not just one person
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, sukun on ع, and ُ at end
Added سَ at beginning to show future tense

steps.m: and he will know (what will happen to those who wronged)
steps.n: Added وَ to connect to previous statement. This word connects to the next phrase about wrongdoers.
Starting with the basic demonstrative letter ذ and adding ي to make it into a relative pronoun
Adding ال makes it definite and changes it from a general 'who' to 'the one who'
The ل gets a shadda and the initial alif becomes a wasl alif (ٱ) for pronunciation

steps.m: those who (referring to the believers mentioned in ءَامَنُوا)
steps.n: Adding ن makes it plural, changing from 'the one who' to 'those who'. This refers to the believers (المؤمنين) mentioned in the verse.

steps.m: to wrong, oppress, or act unjustly
steps.n: This is the root meaning showing the basic concept of wronging or oppressing
Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added وا at the end to show a group of people did this action
Added pronunciation marks (the small و above the alif) to help with recitation. This doesn't change the meaning.
Basic noun pattern with fatha-sukoon vowels
Adding shadda makes the word stronger and more definite in meaning
The مُن prefix changes this into a noun meaning 'place or time of turning'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Added مُنْ at the beginning to show where or when something happens. This pattern is used to show the place or time an action occurs.

steps.m: a place/time of returning (in the ayah: the destination they will return to)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra (ٍ) at the end to show this is indefinite (like 'a' in English) and is being affected by something else in the sentence
This root carries the basic meaning of turning, returning, or changing from one state to another
The ن makes it mean something happens on its own or to oneself
Added ون to show many people are doing this action
